consistency.notes: All non-null, non-empty ground-truth labels are supported by the page text. Product title, roaster, location, origin, process, varieties, producer, altitude, roast, harvest, tasting notes, price, currency, bag size, package grams, availability, and statements that it is not a blend and not decaffeinated are explicit. 'is_espresso': false is supported indirectly because the product is presented as a Guatemala single-origin wholesale coffee with roast/use notes for filter/batch brew, while a separate 'Espresso Blend Wholesale 5 lb' product is listed elsewhere. 'roaster_country': 'United States' follows from the Richmond, Virginia address/location. 'is_specialty_coffee': true is consistent with the specialty coffee roaster/product context. 'is_coferment_or_infused': false is supported by the straightforward washed process description with no mention of co-fermentation or infusion.

realism.reasons: Very plausible as a real specialty coffee roaster wholesale product page. The structure matches common Shopify-style roaster pages: header utility links, product title, price, options, stock note, spec bullets, descriptive copy, wholesale notes, related products, and footer. The coffee details are coherent and industry-appropriate: Finca La Bolsa in La Libertad, Huehuetenango; washed Bourbon/Caturra; 1500–1700 masl; seasonal harvest timing; and tasting notes that fit a washed Guatemala. The voice is restrained and commercial rather than overly flowery, which reads authentic for a wholesale listing. Pricing also feels believable for a 5 lb wholesale bag, including quantity breaks. The variant block is internally consistent: 5 lb equals 2268 g, SKUs look realistic, and grind variants sharing price is normal. Minor tiny tells: 'This lot is a straightforward washed separation of Bourbon and Caturra' is slightly odd phrasing, and 'medium-light filter roast' on a wholesale page could be a touch more standardized, but neither is enough to make it feel synthetic.

label.coffee.sensory_text: Tasting notes: red apple, milk chocolate, almond, orange blossom
label.coffee.display_tasting_notes: red apple, milk chocolate, almond, orange blossom
label.coffee.producer_text: La Bolsa sits outside La Libertad in Huehuetenango, where cool nights and limestone-rich soils make for a structured, sweet cup. This lot is a straightforward washed separation of Bourbon and Caturra, selected for cafe batch brew and retail refill bins where consistency matters.
